description: A medium-sized blue damselfly from the Pontic region (Black Sea coast). Males are blue with black markings. It is closely related to *Coenagrion puella* and was for a long time considered a subspecies or synonym.
behaviour: Found near vegetated still and slow-flowing waters in its Black Sea coastal range.
distribution: *Coenagrion ponticum* occurs along the Black Sea coast, including parts of Turkey, Georgia, and possibly Ukraine.
habitat: Vegetated margins of ponds, lakes, and slow-flowing waters.
sources: Atlas of the European Dragonflies and Damselflies, Jean-Pierre Boudot(Editor), Vincent J Kalkman(Editor), Fons Peels(Illustrator)
Field guide to the dragonflies of Britain and Europe, Klaas-Douwe B Dijkstra.
